overview.text: NSW Ambulance provides emergency medical response, aeromedical retrieval, rescue operations and non-emergency transport across more than 800,000 km² of New South Wales. Over 1.2 million incidents are handled yearly.

items: Serves 7.9+ million people across metro, regional and remote NSW.
Road fleet travels ~40–45 million km per year.
One of the largest ambulance fleets in the world (around 1,500 vehicles).
{b:Clinical Operations:} front-line road paramedics and specialist roles.
{b:Special Operations Team (SOT) / RAA:} remote & complex rescue environments.
{b:Critical Care:} CCPs, flight nurses & retrieval doctors.
Ambulance Control Centres in Sydney, Newcastle, Wollongong and Dubbo.
Aeromedical Control Centre for helicopter and fixed-wing dispatch.
CAD / MDT and GPS-based nearest resource assignment.

sections.intro: NSW Ambulance category codes and whether they usually run lights and sirens.

sections.title: 🎧 Common Radio Phrases
sections.intro: Common terms you will hear on NSW Ambulance talkgroups.

sections.title: 📡 Talkgroups & Monitoring Notes
items: Each NSW Ambulance region has one or more {b:primary dispatch} talkgroups.
{b:Aeromedical} uses dedicated or shared retrieval channels.
Major incidents may link FRNSW, RFS, SES, Police on shared or patched channels.
Some talkgroups may be encrypted or carry limited traffic.
Traffic volume increases during storms, heatwaves and major events.
Do not publish identifiable patient information.
